title: Problems
summary: This page contains end-of-chapter problems from Chapter 1 related to signals and systems, including figures for problems P1.1-12, P1.2-1, P1.2-2, and P1.2-3. Problems involve plotting signals, analyzing time inversion, time shifting, time compression, and time expansion effects on signal energy. A specific problem asks students to sketch and analyze the signal x(t) = 2^(-t)u(t).

figures.caption: A collection of five continuous-time signals x(t), x₁(t), x₂(t), x₃(t), x₄(t), and x₅(t) with various piecewise-linear waveforms used for signal transformation exercises.
figures.description: The figure shows several continuous-time signal plots on horizontal time axes labeled t with vertical amplitude axes labeled x(t) or x_i(t). The signals include a repeating unit-height pulse train, a piecewise-linear waveform with marked times such as 6, 12, 15, and 24, and multiple finite-duration shapes including ramps, triangles, a rectangular pulse, a V-shaped segment, a peaked waveform with negative edge values, and a trapezoid-like pulse. Students should notice the breakpoints, amplitude labels (for example 1, 0.5, -1, 2, and 4), and how each signal is defined by linear segments and jumps over specific time intervals for signal-transformation practice.
